description: Main sociological definitions and formulas that are not part of the units, physics, math or chemistry base data.

words.name: sociology
words.description: social science that studies human society and its development

words.name: criminology
words.description: study of the causes and manifestations of crime

words.name: demography
words.description: science that deals with populations and their structures statistically and theoretically

words.name: fertility
words.description: natural capability to produce offspring

words.name: emigration
words.description: act of leaving one's country or region with the intent to settle permanently or temporarily in another

words.name: immigration
words.description: movement of people into another country or region to which they are not native

words.name: crime
words.description: unlawful act punishable by an authority

words.name: deviance
words.description: action or behavior that violates social norms

words.name: anomie
words.description: normlessness: the lack of social norms

words.name: conformity
words.description: the act of matching attitudes, beliefs, and behaviors to group norms

words.name: urbanization
words.description: longterm population movements (shift) from rural to urban areas

words.name: suburbanization
words.description: growth of suburbs and its areas on the fringes of cities

words.name: gentrification
words.description: urban socioeconomic process

words.name: authority
words.description: legitimate power to decide or authorize

words.name: legitimacy
words.description: right and acceptance of an authority

words.name: bureaucracy
words.description: administrative system governing any large institution

words.name: patriarchy
words.description: social system dominated by men

words.name: caste
words.description: fixed social group into which an individual is born within a particular system of social stratification

words.name: religion
words.description: social-cultural system

words.name: secularization
words.description: process by which society evolves towards secularity

words.name: sect
words.description: subgroup of a particular religious or ideological doctrine

words.name: cult
words.description: social group with socially deviant or novel religious, philosophical or spiritual beliefs and practices

words.name: society
words.description: group of people connected to each other

words.name: solidarity
words.description: unity of feeling or action on a common interest (for the social movement see Q1003)

words.name: modernization
words.description: explanation for the process of modernization within societies

words.name: globalization
words.description: process of international integration arising from world views, products, ideas, and other aspects of culture

words.name: culture
words.description: shared aspects of a society's way of life

words.name: subculture
words.description: social culture within a larger culture

words.name: counterculture
words.description: subculture whose values and norms of behavior deviate from those of mainstream society

words.name: value
words.description: personal value, basis for ethical action

words.name: belief
words.description: psychological state in which an individual holds a proposition or premise to be true

words.name: mores
words.description: customary behaviour

words.name: taboo
words.description: societal or cultural prohibition

words.name: McDonaldization
words.description: sociological term; a metaphor that links the exploitative logic behind globalized modern culture to the principles and practices of running a fast-food outlet

words.name: feminism
words.description: group of movements that come about defining, and defending women liberation and gender equity

words.name: in-group
words.description: a social group to which a person psychologically identifies as being a member

words.name: prejudice
words.description: prejudgment, or forming an opinion before becoming aware of the relevant facts of a case

words.name: discrimination
words.description: differential treatment based on membership of a certain group

words.name: stereotype
words.description: over-generalized belief about a particular category of people; often used in literature and films as means to emphasize characters

words.name: racism
words.description: race or ethnic-based discrimination

words.name: role
words.description: set of behaviours, rights, obligations, beliefs, and norms expected from an individual that has a certain social status

words.name: socialization
words.description: lifelong process of inheriting and disseminating norms, customs and ideologies

words.name: resocialization
words.description: process of reinsertion of former prisoners into society

words.name: dyad
words.description: group of two people, the smallest possible social group

words.name: triad
words.description: sociological term: group of three people

words.name: ethnocentrism
words.description: judging other cultures, practices, behaviors, beliefs and people solely by the values, standards and frame of reference of one's own

words.name: gender
words.description: social concept which distinguish the different gender categories

words.name: race
words.description: nonscientific human classification system primarily based on physical appearance

words.name: ethnicity
words.description: a person's ethnic background

words.name: family
words.description: group of people affiliated by consanguinity, law, affinity, or co-residence

words.name: kinship
words.description: human relationship term; web of social relationships that form an important part of the lives of most humans in most societies; form of social connection

words.name: marriage
words.description: social union or legal contract between people called spouses that creates kinship

words.name: monogamy
words.description: relationship form where each individual has only one partner during their lifetime or at any one time

words.name: polygamy
words.description: marriage to more than one spouse

words.name: divorce
words.description: termination of a marital union

triples.name: social theory
triples.description: analytical framework, or paradigm, that is used to study and interpret social phenomena

triples.name: urban sociology
triples.description: sociological study of life and human interaction in metropolitan areas

triples.name: political sociology
triples.description: branch of sociology

triples.name: economic sociology
triples.description: study of the social cause and effect of various economic phenomena
branch of sociology
branch of sociology

triples.name: medical sociology
triples.description: branch of sociology concerned with sociological analysis of medical organizations and institutions

triples.name: environmental sociology
triples.description: study of interactions between societies and their natural environments

triples.name: industrial sociology
triples.description: branch of the discipline of sociology

triples.name: social psychology
triples.description: area of sociology focused on social actions

triples.name: demographic transition
triples.description: transition from high birth and death rates to lower birth and death rates as a country or region develops from a pre-industrial to an industrialized economic system

triples.name: mortality rate
triples.description: measure of the number of deaths in a population from a given cause, scaled by population, in a set period of time

triples.name: human migration
triples.description: movement of people for resettlement

triples.name: life expectancy
triples.description: statistical measure of how long a person or organism may live, based on factors of their life

triples.name: population density
triples.description: measurement of population per unit area or unit volume

triples.name: infant mortality
triples.description: statistical concept of infant deaths

triples.name: dependency ratio
triples.description: age-population ratio of those typically not in the labor force (the dependent part ages 0 to 14 and 65+) and those typically in the labor force (the productive part ages 15 to 64)

triples.name: sex ratio
triples.description: ratio of males to females in a population

triples.name: social control
triples.description: concept within the disciplines of the social sciences and within political science

triples.name: social stigma
triples.description: type of discrimination or disapproval based on perceivable social characteristics that serve to distinguish a person from other members of a society

triples.name: social power
triples.description: ability to influence the behavior of others

triples.name: social movement
triples.description: loosely organized effort by a large group of people to achieve a particular set of goals

triples.name: collective behavior
triples.description: social processes and events which do not reflect existing social structure, but which emerge in a "spontaneous" way

triples.name: social class
triples.description: group of people categorized in a hierarchy based on socioeconomic factors

triples.name: social stratification
triples.description: society's categorization of its people into hierarchical groups based on socioeconomic factors

triples.name: social mobility
triples.description: mobility to move social classes

triples.name: social inequality
triples.description: uneven distribution of resources in a society

triples.name: social capital
triples.description: sociology concept

triples.name: cultural capital
triples.description: sociological concept

triples.name: religious denomination
triples.description: identifiable religious subgroup with a common structure and doctrine

triples.name: social structure
triples.description: aggregate of patterned social arrangements in society

triples.name: social institution
triples.description: structure or mechanism of social order and cooperation governing the behaviour of a set of individuals within a given community

triples.name: social fact
triples.description: a category of facts, defined by Emile Durkheim, that consist of the ways of acting, thinking and feeling external to the individual and endowed with coercive power over them
separation of tasks in any system (particularly the society) so that participants may specialize

triples.name: symbolic interactionism
triples.description: a sociological theory focused on cultural symbols exchanged during interpersonal interactions

triples.name: structural functionalism
triples.description: a sociological theory arguing that the stability of society is determined by functional institutions and individuals having a specific role

triples.name: conflict theory
triples.description: perspectives in sociology and social psychology that emphasize a materialist interpretation of history, dialectical method of analysis, a critical stance toward existing social arrangements, and political program of revolution or, at least, reform

triples.name: collective consciousness
triples.description: shared beliefs and ideas in society

triples.name: social change
triples.description: any significant alteration in societal order

triples.name: social network
triples.description: theoretical concept in sociology

triples.name: cultural relativism
triples.description: principle that cultures should be understood and evaluated on their own terms and not by universal or outsider standards

triples.name: social norm
triples.description: informal understanding of acceptable conduct

triples.name: social identity
triples.description: qualities, beliefs, personality, looks and/or expressions that distinguish a person or group

triples.name: reference group
triples.description: social groups

triples.name: role conflict
triples.description: lack of compatibility between different expectations from a job or position

triples.name: social status
triples.description: position within social structure

triples.name: ascribed status
triples.description: social status based on immutable characteristics

triples.name: achieved status
triples.description: social position achieved by actions

triples.name: social group
triples.description: two or more humans who interact with one another

triples.name: social cohesion
triples.description: bonding between members of a group

triples.name: social integration
triples.description: social incorporation of outgroups

triples.name: gender role
triples.description: social role encompassing a range of behaviors and attitudes that are generally considered acceptable, appropriate, or desirable for people based on their actual or perceived sex or sexuality

triples.name: minority group
triples.description: group of people by practices, race, religion, ethnicity, or other characteristics who are fewer in numbers than the main groups of those classifications

triples.name: nuclear family
triples.description: family group consisting of parents and their children (one or more)

triples.name: extended family
triples.description: a joint family is a traditional family structure where multiple generations, like grandparents, parents, and children, live together under one roof
the number of live births in a population during a given period
the number of deaths in a population during a given period
the number of people moving into an area during a given period
the number of people leaving an area during a given period

triples.name: infant deaths
triples.description: the number of deaths of children under one year of age during a given period

triples.name: live births
triples.description: the number of children born alive during a given period

triples.name: dependent population
triples.description: the part of a population that is younger than the working age or older than it

triples.name: working-age population
triples.description: the part of a population that is of working age, typically 15 to 64 years

triples.name: male population
triples.description: the number of males in a population

triples.name: female population
triples.description: the number of females in a population

triples.name: land area
triples.description: the size of the land surface of an area

triples.name: crude birth rate
triples.description: the number of live births per 1000 people in a population per year

triples.name: crude death rate
triples.description: the number of deaths per 1000 people in a population per year
the difference between the crude birth rate and the crude death rate of a population

triples.name: net migration rate
triples.description: the difference between the number of immigrants and emigrants per 1000 people per year

triples.name: replacement fertility rate
triples.description: the total fertility rate at which a population exactly replaces itself from one generation to the next, about 2.1 children per woman
a rule of thumb that estimates the number of years for a quantity to double by dividing 70 by its percentage growth rate

formulas.name: definition of rate of natural increase
formulas.expression: "rate of natural increase" = ( "number of births" - "number of deaths" ) / "population" * 1000

formulas.name: definition of net migration rate
formulas.expression: "net migration rate" = ( "number of immigrants" - "number of emigrants" ) / "population" * 1000

formulas.name: doubling time by the rule of 70

sources.name: by definition
sources.description: a value that is fixed by an unambiguous definition or convention rather than measured

sources.name: sociology reference data
sources.description: typical reference values for human populations as cited in sociology and demography textbooks
